title: Listening - Part 4: Meditation and Mindfulness
description: You will hear five short extracts in which people talk about their experience of practicing mindfulness or meditation. You must complete two tasks while listening.

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I’m a professional athlete, and [21]I was encouraged to try meditation by my coach after a series of disappointing performances where I’d clearly lost my focus[/21]. I was sceptical at first. But [26]what I’ve really noticed is how much it’s improved my ability to recover from setbacks[/26]. If I make a mistake now, I don't dwell on it for twenty minutes; I just reset and move on. It’s been a game-changer for my mental toughness. || Speaker 2: I started a mindfulness course because [22]I was finding it impossible to switch off from my high-pressure legal job, and it was starting to affect my relationships[/22]. I was always half-present. The biggest change, though, has been [27]the way I react to small, everyday annoyances like traffic jams or delays[/27]. I used to get so stressed, but now I just breathe and wait. It’s made me a much more patient person to be around. || Speaker 3: I’m a university student, and [23]I took up meditation because I was suffering from terrible insomnia during my final exams[/23]. My brain just wouldn't stop racing at night. It’s helped with the sleep, but [28]the real benefit has been the boost in my overall concentration levels[/28]. I can study for two hours straight without feeling the urge to check my phone every five minutes. My grades have actually improved as a result. || Speaker 4: I’m a surgeon, and [24]I began practicing mindfulness as part of a hospital-wide initiative to reduce burnout among the staff[/24]. We were all exhausted. Surprisingly, [29]I’ve found that it’s made me much more empathetic towards my patients[/29]. I’m better at really listening to their concerns instead of just seeing them as another case to be solved. It’s made me a better doctor, I think. || Speaker 5: I started meditating [25]primarily because I wanted to explore the philosophical side of it after reading a book on Eastern traditions[/25]. I was curious about the history. What I’ve gained, however, is [30]a much better sense of physical self-awareness[/30]. I notice tension in my shoulders or a headache coming on much earlier than I used to, which means I can deal with it before it becomes a real problem. It’s made me much more in tune with my own body.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21 – 25, choose from the list (A – H) why each speaker started meditating.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1's coach encouraged them after 'disappointing performances'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 found it impossible to 'switch off' and it affected their relationships.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 started as part of a 'hospital-wide initiative' to reduce burnout.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 wanted to explore the 'philosophical side' after reading a book.

options.A: to satisfy an interest in philosophy
options.C: to prepare for a stressful public event
options.D: to recover from a physical injury
options.E: to address a dip in professional performance
options.G: to participate in a workplace scheme

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26 – 30, choose from the list (A – H) what each speaker has found most rewarding.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 can 'reset and move on' from setbacks more easily.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 is much more patient with 'everyday annoyances'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 can study longer and has seen 'overall concentration levels' boost.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 is 'much more empathetic' and better at listening to patients.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 has a better sense of 'physical self-awareness' and tension.

options.A: a heightened sensitivity to bodily signals
options.C: a calmer reaction to minor frustrations
options.D: the ability to recover from failures
options.E: a more positive outlook on the future
